cards.title: What is Machine Learning?
lines: Machine learning is a process of using data to improve performance or make predictions.
A model is trained by showing it many examples of data.
Features are the input values that describe each example.
A label is the correct answer that the model is trying to predict.
Training data is used to teach the model, and test data checks how well it learned.
Pattern recognition means finding repeated relationships in a data set.

cards.title: Learning Techniques and Applications
lines: Classification sorts data into set categories, such as spam or not spam.
Clustering groups similar data together without pre-set labels.
Feedback helps improve the accuracy of a model over time.
Spam filters learn to separate wanted emails from unwanted ones.
Recommendation systems suggest items based on past user choices.
Tools like Teachable Machine let beginners train models without heavy coding.

cards.title: Core Programming Concepts
lines: A variable stores a value that can change while a program runs.
A conditional uses IF and ELSE to make decisions based on a test.
A loop repeats a block of instructions using iteration.
A function is a named block of code that performs a specific task.
Sequence, selection, and iteration are the three basic logical structures.
A flowchart or pseudocode can represent the steps of an algorithm clearly.

cards.title: Data Types and Data Structures
lines: An integer is a whole number and a float is a number with a decimal point.
A string is a sequence of characters such as text.
A Boolean can only be True or False.
A list is an ordered collection of values that can be changed.
A dictionary stores data as key and value pairs.
A graph stores data as nodes connected by edges, useful for networks and routes.

cards.title: Rule-Based Systems and Logic
lines: A rule-based system uses set rules to make decisions.
Each rule has a condition and an action written as IF condition THEN action.
A decision tree shows choices as branches leading to outcomes.
Logical operators AND, OR, and NOT combine or reverse conditions.
A truth table lists the output for every combination of input conditions.
Rule-based systems struggle to handle uncertainty or very complex data.

cards.title: Planning and Autonomous Agents
lines: An autonomous system operates without direct human control.
An AI agent generates a plan based on its goals and available actions.
Conditional logic lets an agent choose actions based on its environment.
Sensors provide input data that influences the agent's decisions.
If the environment changes, the plan may need to be adjusted.
Examples include self-driving cars, robot vacuum cleaners, and smart thermostats.

cards.title: Ethical Issues in AI
lines: Bias happens when training data is unfair, leading to unfair AI decisions.
Privacy is at risk when personal data is used without informed consent.
Transparency means being able to see how an AI reached a decision.
Accountability means someone is responsible for an AI system's actions.
Facial recognition and surveillance raise concerns about personal freedom.
Developers, companies, and users all share responsibility for ethical AI use.

cards.title: Social and Personal Impact of AI
lines: AI can improve convenience and access to services such as healthcare and education.
AI may cause job displacement as machines take over some tasks.
Recommendation algorithms can influence the choices people make.
AI can affect privacy, personal data, and digital autonomy.
Different groups in society may be affected by AI unequally.
AI has both positive and negative effects at the individual and societal level.

cards.title: Building, Testing and Deploying AI Systems
lines: Building an AI system starts with defining the problem to be solved.
Data is selected and prepared before choosing and training a model.
Accuracy is a performance metric that measures how often the model is correct.
Test data must be kept separate from training data for a fair evaluation.
AI systems can be deployed as web apps, APIs, or cloud-based platforms.
Documentation and version control help others use and maintain the system.
